items.title: The Housemaid author Freida McFadden reveals her real name
items.summary_auto: It turns out the biggest twist in Freida McFadden’s career isn’t in her books. The bestselling author, known for keeping readers guessing, is revealing a surprise of her own: Her real name is Sara Cohen.

items.title: Poilievre holds news conference in B.C.
items.summary_auto: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre holds a news conference about property rights in Richmond B.C. and takes questions from journalists a day after another Conservative MP crossed the floor to the Liberals.

items.title: Former chief Crown attorney sues N.S. alleging bullying, unjust demotion in lawsuit
items.summary_auto: A former chief Crown attorney is suing the Nova Scotia government and the province’s Public Prosecution Service, alleging she was bullied, sidelined without explanation and ultimately forced out of her leadership role.

items.title: Soccer fans give 'stupid, stupid' 2026 World Cup jerseys the cold shoulder
items.summary_auto: Fans love the designs of Nike's new World Cup soccer jerseys. They do not love the way they bunch at the shoulders.

items.title: Gas flaring at LNG Canada far exceeds permitted volume, documents show
items.summary_auto: Documents show the volume of gas flared at the LNG Canada plant on B.C. 's North Coast between October and January far surpassed what its permit allows.

items.title: Police note-taking during prep meetings with complainants 'unacceptably negligent': Stronach defence
items.summary_auto: Poor and incomplete note-taking by police during preparatory meetings with the complainants and Crown prosecutors in the sexual assault trial of Frank Stronach was "unacceptably negligent," Stronach's defence lawyer argued on Thursday.

items.title: Air Canada is testing a new program to resolve customer complaints. Will it work?
Man involved in $22M gold heist from Pearson airport sentenced to 4 years in prison
items.summary_auto: A 43-year-old man has been sentenced to four years in prison in connection to the multi-million-dollar gold heist at Pearson airport in 2023.

items.title: Lawyers for Sean 'Diddy' Combs appeal his conviction with First Amendment argument
items.summary_auto: The hip-hop mogul is challenging his conviction and more than four-year prison sentence. In court on Thursday morning, lawyers for Combs are poised to argue that the rapper was treated unfairly during his high-profile trial last year, and that the First Amendment should win him his freedom.

items.title: K-pop supergroup BTS kick off world tour in South Korea after 4-year hiatus
items.summary_auto: The boys of BTS are returning to the stage after a nearly four-year hiatus, during which the members completed their mandatory military service. Despite pouring rain in Seoul, fans packed a stadium to watch the K-pop group kick off their long-awaited world tour.

items.title: She travelled 900 km for an abortion. Years later, Quebec access issues remain urgent
Israel launches fresh strikes on Lebanon as truce talks to start in Pakistan
items.summary_auto: Israel bombed more targets in Lebanon on Thursday, putting the Middle East ceasefire in further jeopardy after its most intensive strikes of the war on its neighbour killed more than 300 people, according to Lebanon's Health Ministry.

items.title: Charge squander prime chance, now face uphill battle to make PWHL post-season
items.summary_auto: The Charge, who have now lost three straight, had the chance to climb into the fourth and final playoff spot in the PWHL standings, but fell 5-3 to the last-place Seattle Torrent on Wednesday. Seattle was playing the second game of a back-to-back set.

items.title: Nancy Garapick, Canadian Olympic swimming medallist who set world record as teen, dead at 64
items.summary_auto: The Halifax native set a world record as a 13-year-old in the 200-metre backstroke on April 27, 1975, at the Eastern Canadian Swimming Championships in Brantford, Ont., while a member of the Halifax Trojan Aquatic Club.

items.title: Woman who died in Ontario hospital shouldn’t have been in wait room, needed frequent assessment, inquest told
items.summary_auto: On the day she died of sepsis, Heather Winterstein shouldn't have been placed in the emergency room waiting area at the St. Catharines hospital until she could see a doctor, an emergency medicine expert told a coroner's inquest Wednesday.

items.title: Heather Winterstein shouldn’t have been in wait room, needed frequent checks, medical expert tells inquest
items.summary_auto: On the day she died of sepsis, Heather Winterstein shouldn't have been placed in the emergency room waiting area at the St. Catharines hospital until she could see a doctor, an emergency medicine expert told a coroner's inquest Wednesday.

items.title: Alberta government faces another legal fight to keep supervised consumption sites open
items.summary_auto: Less than a year after a judge ruled on a similar case in Red Deer, the provincial government is facing an injunction seeking to stop supervised consumption services from closing in Calgary and Lethbridge.

items.title: Award-winning documentary Agatha's Almanac captures 92-year-old's life on ancestral Manitoba farm
items.summary_auto: Amalie Atkins's award-winning documentary offers a glimpse into the colour and texture of the life of her 92-year-old aunt, Agatha Bock, as she works an ancestral farm in southern Manitoba.

items.title: As another firefighting season nears, northern Manitoba mayors hope for more equipment
items.summary_auto: Preparations for the upcoming wildfire season are underway as the snow melts in Manitoba, but some mayors in the province's north say they need more help.

items.title: Iran clamping down in Strait of Hormuz amid tenuous ceasefire
items.summary_auto: The Middle East ceasefire appeared shaky less than two days after it was announced by U.S. President Donald Trump, as Israel bombed more targets in Lebanon on Thursday and Iran showed little sign of relinquishing its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Why human eyes are better at observing the moon than cameras
items.summary_auto: Human eyes are a wonder of nature. While we've invented the camera to work like our eyes, it's not as good as the real thing, something that was highlighted during Artemis II's lunar flyby.

items.title: Liberals descend on Montreal for convention on the cusp of a majority government
items.summary_auto: Thousands of Liberals are gathering in Montreal for the next few days, buzzing on a fifth floor-crossing and with a sought-after majority government in sight.

items.title: You can now get a prescription for nature access if you live in Hamilton, here's how
items.summary_auto: The PaRx program, an initiative that allows healthcare providers to prescribe nature passes to patients, is now available for Hamilton Conservation Authority (HCA) sites.

items.title: Trump and Iran remain far apart on peace terms. Here are the biggest gaps
items.summary_auto: Despite U.S. President Donald Trump's sunny assessment of the prospects for a long-term peace agreement with Iran, there are fundamental differences in the visions the two sides have put forward for a deal.

items.title: Volkswagen Beetle removed from rock face in Squamish, B.C.
items.summary_auto: A group claiming to represent a group of anonymous University of B.C. engineering students took credit for suspending the car last week, as part of a long-running tradition of pranks.

items.title: For more than 4 decades, a mega-music fan recorded over 10,000 concerts. Now many are online
items.summary_auto: Although Aadam Jacobs concert-recording career has come to an end, his love of music and recordings continues to live online, acting as a valuable entity to the music community.

items.title: Survivor of intimate partner violence asks P.E.I. MLAs for better protection for victims
items.summary_auto: Clare’s Law allows police officers to provide an individual’s history of abusive behaviour to those who may be at risk. It was named after Clare Wood, a 36-year-old woman in the U.K. who was murdered by a former domestic partner who police knew to be dangerous.
